items.title: 'Why would I do that?': Boy in court charged with murdering mother, stabbing sister

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

Police stand around on a lawn of a house

Police stand around on a lawn of a house

items.title: West Aussie Kennedy to carry flag for Glasgow opening ceremony

children.content: Reigning Commonwealth Games gold medallists Nina Kennedy and Tim Hodge will carry the Australian flag and King's baton at the Glasgow 2026 opening ceremony.

image.altText: Nina Kennedy holds the Australian flag behind her.

items.title: Doctor reveals injuries of woman allegedly sexually assaulted by Yung Filly

children.content: Graphic photographs of injuries allegedly inflicted by an internationally renowned YouTube star on an infatuated fan in his hotel room after a concert are shown to a jury at his sexual assault trial in a Perth court.

image.altText: A man with African appearence leaves a court building.

items.title: Rusted tin transformed into prize-winning portraits

children.content: Pilbara artist Jenna Pickering transforms found bush objects into hyper-realistic portraits as artist-in-residence at the remote Cossack Art Awards.

gallery.altText: Detailed painting of a man on a piece of tin.
A woman holds a piece of metal above her head and makes a funny face in front of an old building.
A woman painting laughs as two women behind her laugh.

thumbnail.altText: Detailed painting of a man on a piece of tin.
A woman holds a piece of metal above her head and makes a funny face in front of an old building.
A woman painting laughs as two women behind her laugh.

items.title: 'These things didn't happen': Senior Catholic cleric denies sex abuse

children.content: The lawyer for a former Catholic bishop who allegedly sexually abused three young men tells his trial the incidents did not happen, and warns the jury not to be affected by possible prejudice against the church.

image.altText: A mid-shot of former Bishop of Broome, Christopher Saunders, outside court wearing a dark coat, grey scarf and grey flat cap.

items.title: Twelve dolphins now found dead in southern WA

children.content: Twelve pantropical spotted dolphins have died after a pod was first found stranded on an Albany beach on Tuesday afternoon.

image.altText: A dead dolphin lying on rocks beside the water

items.title: Minister says Australian IS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can 'rot' in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

image.altText: Dr Tareq Kamleh

items.title: Man faces court over alleged Goldfields murder attempt

children.content: Police allege the 42-year-old stabbed a woman multiple times with a knife while dealing drugs on Kalgoorlie's main street.

image.altText: A forensics officer holding a camera.

items.title: WA man dies after not being able to get antipsychotic medication

children.content: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

gallery.altText: Bradley Buswell smiles wearing a collared shirt.

thumbnail.altText: Bradley Buswell smiles wearing a collared shirt.

items.title: Labor to extend paid placements to more care industry students

children.content: Anthony Albanese will announce an expansion of the Commonwealth's "paid prac" scheme at Labor's national conference. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese

items.title: OpenAI model goes rogue during testing and hacks startup

children.content: OpenAI has announced one of its AI models broke containment during testing before hacking into another AI startup.

image.altText: Sam Altman standing in front of the OpenAI logo.

items.title: Labor's members to debate gas and gambling

children.content: Anthony Albanese may have been a keen participant in robust conference debates in years gone by but is now using his authority to enforce a much more controlled process.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag in a grey suit and blue tie looking serious

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: The 'fairy gold dust passport' more Australians are chasing

children.content: Australians are racing to secure Irish citizenship, with new figures showing applications have more than doubled in the past three years as people seek the right to live, work and study across the EU.

image.altText: A family of four taking a selfie infront of the colloseum in Rome

items.title: 'Massive' drop in tourists this winter hits WA's south

children.content: Despite being an idyllic location for a winter getaway, businesses in WA's Great Southern have recorded a decline in tourists this season.

items.title: ASX set to open higher as Tesla shares plunge

children.content: The Australian share market is set to open in the green after Tesla shares plunging on cash flow news. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close-up of the Tesla emblem on a grey car. June 2025

items.title: Nintendo raised prices due to US tariffs. Now, it doesn't want to pass on refunds

children.content: Nintendo has argued it has no duty to pass its refunds onto US consumers. It's the latest in a lengthy tariff fight.

image.altText: A handheld gaming device.

items.title: How we got to Glasgow 2026 and what the Commonwealth Games will look like

children.content: The bumpy road to Glasgow 2026 has produced a Commonwealth Games that will look a little different than the ones that came before.

image.altText: Swimmer Duncan Scott with 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games mascots Clyde and Finnie.

items.title: Your guide to every day of the Commonwealth Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

items.title: How One Nation's winning over the bush

children.content: One Nation has some of its strongest support in outback Australia and recent polling shows it could win over 30 regional seats. So what's winning over regional Australians when it comes to Pauline Hanson's party?

articles.title: 'Don't go there': Pauline Hanson fires up in Q&A over White Australia policy

children.content: Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

image.altText: Pauline is pictured at a lectern advertising the Leadership Matters event against a blue background.

articles.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

children.content: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson sits on stage talking during a question and answer session at a breakfact function, against a blue background.

items.title: Try our quiz about Australian TV's night of nights, the Logies

children.content: As the Logies approach, on August 16, we thought we would test your knowledge of the beloved but daggy Australian television awards and this year's nominees.

items.title: States can use their own emergency warnings when AusAlert goes live

children.content: State and territory representatives had voiced significant concerns about the rollout of the national emergency warning system in a meeting with the minister Kristy McBain.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

image.altText: Woman at airport

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A close-up shot of the former Bishop of Broome, Christopher Alan Saunders, arriving at court in Perth, in front of another man.

items.title: Police probe use of force on handcuffed teen injured during Perth arrest

children.content: A handcuffed teenager was injured when he was forced to the ground by a police officer, sparking a WA Police review after video of the incident emerged.
